segments.title: Operation Deadlight follows the U-boat surrender
segments.summary: The live lecture frames Operation Deadlight through the May 1945 U-boat scuttlings, Allied surrender terms, and the later disposal of surviving German submarines.
segments.body: The discussion captures the lecture's central transition from German scuttling orders to Allied handling of surrendered U-boats. Dr. Clarke walks through the May 1945 destruction sequence, Montgomery's insistence that German naval forces be surrendered intact, the surviving boats that reached Allied ports, and the later Operation Deadlight sinkings that continued into February 1946.

evidence.note: The transcript dates the first scuttlings from May 1-5, then connects Montgomery's surrender terms with the brief Regenbogen order and further scuttlings.
The lecture counts 150 U-boats left to surrender to the Allies, describes where they arrived, and contrasts mostly home-boat scuttlings with damaged front boats.
The transcript identifies the final February 1946 sinking anniversary and says Operation Deadlight began sinking boats in May 1945 and finished in February 1946.
